bullets.text: Growth measures described as the most disappointing in some time; rate reductions of 2–4% on average auto premium per policy not yet fully reflected in new business response late in the quarter.
Underwriting profit margin of 10.5% remained strong despite seasonal storm frequency mid-quarter; management views margin gap to 96 combined ratio target as beginning to close slowly.

Total PIF grew about 220,000 or 2.2% from year-end 2006 and 3% year-over-year; Direct personal auto PIF up 5% year-over-year with early signs of improving retention from rate actions; retention and policy life expectancy increased in almost every tier.

Average premium reduced 2–4% across the book with selected segments seeing wider cuts; management expects deflationary premium-per-policy effect to persist for some time as mix and rate changes flow through.

First TV commercials promoting the concierge claims offering aired with positive effect on consumer awareness; over 50 centers now operational and positioned for broader marketing with high customer satisfaction and retention measures.

New business application growth returned in half of all states for both Agency and Direct by quarter-end; Florida, Texas, and Virginia identified as key large states not meeting growth objectives and receiving focused attention.

E-fulfillment availability expanded for paperless auto policy delivery; paperless billing options well received; early pilot tested tree-planting reward for customers enrolling in paperless in selected markets.
